questions.prompt: Complete: "The solar panels were installed last spring. ___, the building's energy bills fell by 30%."
questions.explanation: The solar panels caused the bills to fall — a direct result. "Consequently" is the correct result marker here. "Nevertheless" introduces a concession (something happening despite an obstacle) — no obstacle is mentioned. "Whereas" joins two contrasting clauses in a single sentence and cannot open a new sentence. "On the contrary" denies the previous statement and asserts the opposite — inappropriate when the second sentence is a natural consequence.

options.text: Despite the heavy traffic, she arrived on time.
Despite the fact that traffic was heavy, she arrived on time.
Despite traffic being heavy, she arrived on time.
Despite traffic was heavy, she arrived on time.

questions.explanation: Option (d) is wrong: "Despite" cannot be followed directly by a subject + finite verb (traffic was heavy). After "Despite" use a noun phrase (option a), "the fact that" + clause (option b), or a gerund phrase (option c — "traffic being heavy"). All three correct options use grammatically valid structures after "despite"; option (d) does not.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The documentary was long and slow-paced. ___, it offered a profound meditation on loss."
questions.explanation: The second sentence concedes that despite the film's weaknesses (long and slow-paced), it had genuine value — a classic concessive relationship. "Nonetheless" (meaning even so / despite that) is correct. "Furthermore" adds a point in the same direction — wrong here, as the two sentences pull in opposite directions. "Therefore" signals a result — a slow documentary does not logically result in profundity. "Namely" introduces a specification or list — not applicable here.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The scheme was costly, ___ effective in reducing reoffending rates."
questions.explanation: "Albeit" + adjective/adverb/noun phrase is the correct mid-clause concessive structure: costly, albeit effective. It acknowledges the cost while asserting the effectiveness within a single clause. "Although" requires a full finite clause (subject + verb) after it — it cannot precede a bare adjective like "effective". "Nevertheless" and "however" are sentence connectors that must introduce a new clause, not modify within the same clause.

options.text: The committee voted to extend the deadline, thereby the project was saved.
The committee voted to extend the deadline; thereby, they saved the project.
The committee voted to extend the deadline, thereby saving the project.
The committee voted to extend the deadline. Thereby the project was saved.

questions.explanation: "Thereby" must be followed by a present participle (-ing form) to embed a result in the same clause: thereby saving the project. Option (a) follows "thereby" with a full clause (the project was saved) — this is the classic error. Option (b) uses a semicolon and treats "thereby" like "therefore" — incorrect, as "thereby" does not introduce an independent clause. Option (d) starts a new sentence with "Thereby" — it cannot stand as a sentence connector the way "therefore" or "consequently" can.

questions.prompt: Read: "Critics praised the novel's structure. ___, the characterisation was seen as underdeveloped. ___ the author was shortlisted for three awards."  Choose the pair that best fits both gaps.
questions.explanation: Gap 1: the second sentence introduces a weakness contrasting with the praise — "However" (contrast) is correct. Gap 2: despite mixed reviews, the author was still shortlisted — a concession: "Nevertheless" (even so) is correct. Option (a) uses "Moreover" (addition in the same direction) for a contrast — wrong. Option (c) makes the characterisation criticism a result of praise — illogical. Option (d): "On the contrary" would deny the praise entirely, but the critics did praise the structure — the issue is only with characterisation, not a full denial.
